title_original: Optical switch protocol verifies entangled quantum states in real time without destroying them
summary_original: Researchers at the University of Vienna, led by Philip Walther, have developed a novel protocol using active optical switches to verify entangled quantum states in real time without destroying all available states. Published in Science Advances (2026), the method allows only a random subset of generated states to be measured for certification, preserving the remainder for quantum tasks. This efficient, scalable approach reduces resource requirements compared to conventional methods and enables device-independent certification, increasing practical robustness and security. Co-first authors Michael Antesberger, Lee Rozema, and Mariana Schmid emphasize its significance for benchmarking large-scale quantum networks and photonic quantum computers, making it a key step toward practical and secure quantum technologies. (DOI: 10.1126/sciadv.aea4144)
